recurringPattern: Circular avatar (often with a status dot overlay), name in bold, role/title in subdued text, location or meta info in smaller type, and action buttons (Message, Follow, Connect) in outlined style. Card has rounded corners, white background, subtle shadow.
designHarm: The profile card pattern mirrors social media regardless of context — an enterprise directory, a team page, and a dating app get the same layout. The avatar-as-primary-element communicates that appearance matters most, which may be inappropriate for professional contexts. The action button row presumes relationships before users have context to decide.
legitimateExceptions: Social platforms where profile discovery is the core interaction
Team directory pages where quick-contact is the primary task
possibleFixtures: Standard profile card with avatar + actions
Text-only profile card for professional directories
Expanded profile layout with context before actions
originalObservations: AI-generated profile cards always use circular avatars even for contexts where photos aren't available (generating generic placeholder faces).
The online status dot is always a small green circle overlaid at the bottom-right of the avatar.
Action buttons are always outlined/secondary style rather than letting the user explore the profile first.
